zh.lifeSummary: 當James M. Cartwright於1844年6月出生在美國印第安納州克拉克時，他的父親Charles Cartwright24歲，母親Cynthia Ann Ryan17歲。他於1866年12月21日與Alice C. Irene Tresenriter在美國印第安納州哈里森結婚。他們至少是5個女兒的父母。他於1880年住在美國阿肯色州克萊歐亞可 布盧福福 堂史普且於1900年住在美國阿肯色州波爾克。他去世於1903年享年59歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当James M. Cartwright于1844年6月出生在美国印第安纳州克拉克时，他的父亲Charles Cartwright24岁，母亲Cynthia Ann Ryan17岁。他于1866年12月21日与Alice C. Irene Tresenriter在美国印第安纳州哈里森结婚。他们至少是5个女儿的父母。他于1880年住在美国阿肯色州克萊歐亞可 布盧福福 堂史普且于1900年住在美国阿肯色州波爾克。他去世于1903年享年59岁。

nameMeanings.name: Cartwright
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Midlands and northern England): occupational name from Middle English cart(e) ‘cart’ + wright(e) ‘craftsman’ (Old English cræt or Old Norse kartr + Old English wyrhta), for a cartwright, a maker of carts.
Variant of   Courtright  , a surname of Dutch origin.

variants.name: Gathright
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Cartwright  .

variants.name: Cartright
meanings: Probably also a variant of   Courtright  , a surname of Dutch origin.

variants.name: Courtright
meanings: Americanized form of Dutch Kortrijk: habitational name from the city of Kortrijk in Flanders, in French known as Courtrai. Compare Cartwright , Cortright , Courtwright , Curtright , Cutright , and Kortright .
Perhaps also an altered form of English   Cartwright  .

History: The progenitors of the (majority of the) Courtrights (and Cortrights, Kortrights, etc.) were the brothers Jan and Michiel Bastiansen van Kortryk from Leerdam in the Netherlands, who arrived with their families to New Amsterdam in New Netherland (now New York City, NY) in 1663. The descendants of one or another brother also bear the surnames Kortright, Kortwright, Cortright, Co(u)rtwright, Cartwright, and Cu(r)tright.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.description: U.S. acquires vast tracts of Mexican territory in wake of Mexican War including California and New Mexico.

Historical Boundaries
1851: Pemiscot was created from New Madrid County
Abraham Lincoln issues Emancipation Proclamation, declaring slaves in Confederate states to be free.
Historical Boundaries - 1873: Clayton, Arkansas, United States; 1876: *Clay, Arkansas, United States. [*Renamed to Clay from Clayton]

historicEvents.title: A Treaty with Hawaii
historicEvents.description: In the Mid 1870s, The United States sought out the Kingdom of Hawaii to make a free trade agreement. The Treaty gave the Hawaiians access to the United States agricultural markets and it gave the United States a part of land which later became Pearl Harbor.

historicEvents.title: The Mosaic Templar is Founded
historicEvents.description: The Mosaic Templar is an African American fraternal organization founded in Little Rock. it was founded by former slaves, John Edward Bush and Chester W. Keatts. It was part of a movement that was going on at the time, where everyone was forming fraternities and sororities. The main departments for this one where endowment, monument, analysis, uniform, rank, recapitulation, records, and a juvenile division.
historicEvents.urlTitle: The Encyclopedia of Arkansas History & Culture- Mosaic Templars of America

historicEvents.title: The Sherman Antitrust Act
historicEvents.description: This Act tried to prevent the raising of prices by restricting trade. The purpose of the Act was to preserve a competitive marketplace to protect consumers from abuse.

historicEvents.title: Woman's Suffrage
historicEvents.description: An organization formed in favor of women's suffrages. By combining the National Woman Suffrage Association and the American Woman Suffrage Association, the NAWSA eventually increased in membership up to two million people. It is still one of the largest voluntary organizations in the nation today and held a major role in passing the Nineteenth Amendment.
historicEvents.urlTitle: The National American Woman Suffrage Association

en.lifeSummary: When James M. Cartwright was born in June 1844, in Clark, Indiana, United States, his father, Charles Cartwright, was 24 and his mother, Cynthia Ann Ryan, was 17. He married Alice C. Irene Tresenriter on 21 December 1866, in Harrison, Indiana, United States. They were the parents of at least 5 daughters. He lived in Oak Bluff Township, Clay, Arkansas, United States in 1880 and Polk, Arkansas, United States in 1900. He died in 1903, at the age of 59.

ja.lifeSummary: James M. Cartwrightが1844年6月にアメリカ合衆国インディアナ州クラークで生まれた時，父Charles Cartwrightは24歳，母Cynthia Ann Ryanは17歳であった。1866年12月21日にアメリカ合衆国インディアナ州ハーリソンでAlice C. Irene Tresenriterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の娘の親であった。彼は1880年にはアメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州クレイオアク ブルッフ トウンシュイップに，1900年にはアメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州ポルクに住んでいた。彼は1903年に59歳で死去した。

ko.New Albany City, Floyd, Indiana, United: 미국 인디애나 주 플로이드 뉴 알바니
ko.Oak Bluff Township, Clay, Arkansas, Unit: 미국 아칸소 주 클레이 크 블루프 타운시프
ko.lifeSummary: James M. Cartwright(이)가 1844년 6월 미국 인디애나 주 클라르크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Charles Cartwright(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Cynthia Ann Ryan(은)는 17세였다. 그는 1866년 12월 21일 미국 인디애나 주 해리슨에서 Alice C. Irene Tresenriter(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 1880년도에 미국 아칸소 주 클레이 크 블루프 타운시프에서, 1900년도에 미국 아칸소 주 폴크에서 살았다. 그는 1903년 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда James M. Cartwright родился июнь 1844 г. в местности кларк, Индиана, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени Charles Cartwright было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Cynthia Ann Ryan, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice C. Irene Tresenriter 21 декабря 1866 г. в местности харрисон, Индиана, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 5 дочерей. Он жил в местности оак блуфф тоуншип, клеи, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты в 1880 году и в местности полк, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты в 1900 году. Он умер в 1903 г. в возрасте 59 лет.

es.New Albany City, Floyd, Indiana, United: New Albany, Floyd, Indiana, Estados Unidos
es.Oak Bluff Township, Clay, Arkansas, Unit: Oak Bluff Township, Clay, Arkansas, Estados Unidos
es.lifeSummary: Cuando James M. Cartwright nació en junio de 1844, en Clark, Indiana, Estados Unidos, su papá, Charles Cartwright, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Cynthia Ann Ryan, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Alice C. Irene Tresenriter el 21 de diciembre de 1866, en Harrison, Indiana, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijas. Vivió en Oak Bluff Township, Clay, Arkansas, Estados Unidos en 1880 y en Polk, Arkansas, Estados Unidos en 1900. Murió en 1903, a la edad de 59 años.
